news.title: Education Department rollback undermines Title IX protections
news.summary_en: WASHINGTON — This week, the U.S. Department of Education's Office for Civil Rights (OCR) took the step of affirmatively rescinding provisions of previously negotiated resolution agreements, reversing commitments made with schools to address findings of gender discrimination under Title IX. Title IX was created to ensure that no student would be denied participation in, excluded from, or subjected to discrimination in federally funded education programs on the basis of sex. Such reversals of closed Title IX matters are highly unusual and represent a significant departure from longstanding enforcement practices. NEA President Becky Pringle stated: 'This decision guts long-standing Title IX agreements and strikes at the heart of a 50-year-old civil rights law. Title IX guarantees that all students, regardless of sex—including their gender identity or sexual orientation—can learn in safe, welcoming environments. Rolling back agreements designed to address discrimination puts those protections at risk and raises serious concerns about student safety and equity.'
